data.title: UnitedHealth Exceeds Q1 Revenue and Earnings Expectations
data.description: UnitedHealth reported first-quarter revenue of $111.7 billion, surpassing market expectations of $109.57 billion. According to Jin10, the company's adjusted earnings per share for the first quarter were $7.23, exceeding the anticipated $6.57. UnitedHealth forecasts that its adjusted earnings per share for the full year will be higher than $18.25, compared to the market expectation of $17.86.

data.title: Czech PM Urges EU to Ease Environmental Rules Amid Energy Crisis
data.description: Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babis has urged the European Union to relax its environmental regulations in response to the energy price spikes resulting from the ongoing conflict in Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Babis's call for action as energy costs continue to rise across Europe. The Prime Minister emphasized the need for immediate measures to mitigate the economic impact on EU member states. Babis's appeal comes amid growing concerns over energy security and economic stability in the region. The EU is currently facing pressure to balance its environmental commitments with the urgent need to address the energy crisis.

data.title: Iran has not yet decided whether to resume negotiations with the United States.
data.description: According to Iran's Press TV, Iran has stated that no decision has yet been made regarding resuming negotiations with the United States. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance Launches TradFi Contract Sprint with $1.44 Million Prize Pool
data.description:
Binance announced on X the commencement of its TradFi Contract Sprint, offering participants a chance to share in a substantial prize pool totaling $1.44 million. The event is structured to reward active trading in USDT contracts for gold, silver, and popular stocks and ETFs. Participants can earn rewards based on their trading volume, with higher activity leading to greater incentives.

The TradFi Contract Sprint is divided into weekly competitions, with the first week focusing on gold and silver contracts. During this period, traders have the opportunity to compete for a share of the $360,000 USDT prize pool allocated for each week. This initiative aims to encourage engagement in trading traditional financial contracts on the Binance platform, offering lucrative rewards for those who actively participate.

By engaging in the sprint, traders can maximize their potential earnings through increased trading activity. The event is designed to attract traders interested in diversifying their portfolios with traditional financial instruments, providing them with an opportunity to earn significant rewards. The weekly structure of the competition ensures ongoing excitement and engagement, as participants strive to outperform their peers in trading volume.

This sprint represents a strategic move by Binance to enhance user participation in its platform's offerings, particularly in the realm of traditional finance contracts. The substantial prize pool underscores Binance's commitment to rewarding active traders and fostering a dynamic trading environment. Participants are encouraged to leverage their trading skills to capitalize on the rewards available throughout the duration of the sprint.

data.title: Sources: The Iranian delegation has not yet appeared in Islamabad.
data.description: According to Lebanese television station Al-Mayadeen, Pakistani diplomatic sources stated that no Iranian delegation has been spotted in Islamabad so far. (Jinshi)

data.title: Polymarket Launches Prediction Market on Major Crypto Hack
data.description:
A new prediction market has been launched on Polymarket, focusing on the timing of the next major cryptocurrency hack exceeding $100 million in losses. According to Odaily, this market primarily references the Rekt News leaderboard for adjudication, but it may also rely on other recognized reliable reports. The prediction market has set four key dates: April 30, June 30, September 30, and December 31.

The contract rules specify that the market concerns any crypto project or exchange experiencing an attack or hack valued at a minimum of $100 million between the market's creation and 11:59 p.m. Eastern Time on the specified date. If no such event occurs, the market will conclude with a 'no' decision. Hacks on decentralized exchanges and lending protocols will be included in the assessment.

data.title: Russia Proposes Strict Penalties for Unregistered Cryptocurrency Activities
data.description: Russia's government has introduced a draft bill on the State Duma's website, aiming to impose criminal penalties for organizing digital currency circulation without registration or permission from the Russian Central Bank. According to ChainCatcher, the proposed legislation outlines fines of up to $4,000 and a maximum sentence of four years for ordinary violators. Operators of large cryptocurrency exchanges could face fines up to $13,000, with responsible individuals potentially receiving sentences of five to seven years. The bill also suggests that most cryptocurrency transactions should be conducted through commercial bank apps and proposes penalties for industrial-scale crypto miners who fail to report their activities. If approved by the State Duma and the President, the new regulations are set to take effect on July 1, 2027.

data.title: Bitcoin's Volatility Falls Below Kospi Amid Oil-Price Disruptions
data.description: Bitcoin's 30-day realized volatility has decreased to 42%, which is lower than the Kospi's approximately 51%, according to NS3.AI. This shift follows the Korean benchmark's peak at 74% last week. The fluctuations in the stock market are attributed to disruptions in oil prices around the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Bitcoin has been trading predominantly between $65,000 and $75,000, supported by inflows from U.S. spot ETFs.

data.title: BlackRock CIO Highlights Impact of Stock Buybacks on U.S. Equity Supply
data.description: BlackRock CIO Rick Rieder has highlighted the impact of approximately $1 trillion in stock buybacks this year, which he believes is contributing to a limited supply of equities for buyers. According to NS3.AI, Rieder noted that U.S. stocks are trading near record highs, supported by strong earnings, leadership in the tech sector, and robust high-end consumer spending.

data.title: Polymarket has updated its report, "When will the next cryptocurrency hack costing over $100 million happen?"
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that Polymarket has added a new prediction market titled "When Will the Next Cryptocurrency Hack Worth Over $100 Million Occur?". This market primarily references Rekt News rankings for its judgments, but may also use generally accepted reliable reports. Currently, the prediction market has four dates: April 30th, June 30th, September 30th, and December 31st. The contract rules state that this is a market to determine whether any crypto project or exchange has suffered an attack or hack worth at least $100 million between its creation on this market and 11:59 PM ET on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, the market will ultimately determine "No." Hacks on decentralized exchanges and lending protocols will be included. Odaily Seer continuously monitors prediction markets, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: The Philippine Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has warned that platforms such as dYdX are operating without licenses, and promoters may face criminal charges.
data.description: The Philippine Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has issued an investor warning stating that platforms such as dYdX, Aevo, gTrade, Pacifica, Orderly, Deriv, and Ostium are not registered in the Philippines and have not obtained the necessary licenses to provide cryptocurrency services. The SEC stated that individuals promoting these platforms in the Philippines may face criminal charges, including a fine of up to 5 million Philippine pesos or imprisonment for up to 21 years.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Infini Founder Discusses Stablecoins and AI Agent Integration
data.description:
On April 21, during the 'Decoding Web 4.0: When AI Agents Take Over On-Chain Permissions' roundtable discussion, Infini founder Christian explored the relationship between stablecoins and AI Agents. According to BlockBeats, Christian explained that the underlying logic of stablecoins is not complex, focusing on two main aspects: serving as a substitute for the U.S. dollar to provide a universal currency with settlement and transaction value for global users, and being born and widely used in regions with underdeveloped financial infrastructure. Consequently, the real demand for stablecoins primarily comes from emerging markets rather than developed countries with established financial systems.

Christian emphasized that the greatest value of stablecoins lies in reconstructing payment and settlement efficiency. Compared to the traditional banking system, where single transactions cost between $30–50 and require weeks for integration, current on-chain transaction costs have decreased to a few dollars, enabling micropayments and high-frequency settlements.

He cited Infini's product as an example, noting that users can generate stablecoin payment links within a minute without coding skills and quickly distribute them across various social or business scenarios, achieving instant global payments. This model is nearly impossible in traditional financial systems, where businesses typically face lengthy account opening and payment integration processes.

data.title: Fraudsters Pose as Iranian Authorities Demanding Bitcoin from Stranded Vessels
data.description: MARISKS has issued a warning about fraudsters impersonating Iranian authorities to demand Bitcoin or USDT from stranded vessels. According to NS3.AI, these scammers are exploiting the situation as Iran's legitimate Hormuz toll can reach up to $2 million. Hamid Hosseini informed the Financial Times that Iran's official procedure involves submitting cargo details via email before determining the toll, which is reportedly payable in Bitcoin. The Gulf currently has hundreds of ships and approximately 20,000 seafarers stranded, increasing the number of potential targets for these fraudulent activities.

data.title: Bulgaria's Russia Ties Challenge Anti-Corruption Efforts
data.description: Bulgaria's Prime Minister-elect Rumen Radev faces significant challenges in his plan to combat corruption, primarily due to the country's political and economic connections with Russia. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the complexities Radev may encounter as he seeks to implement reforms. These ties could potentially hinder efforts to establish transparency and accountability within the government. Radev's administration is expected to navigate these geopolitical dynamics carefully to achieve its anti-corruption objectives.

data.title: German Investor Confidence Hits Three-Year Low Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: German investor confidence has plummeted to its lowest point in over three years, as the ongoing conflict in Iran continues to impact Europe's largest economy. Bloomberg posted on X that the geopolitical tensions are undermining the economic recovery efforts in Germany, which had been showing signs of improvement. The decline in investor sentiment reflects growing concerns about the potential economic repercussions of the conflict, which could further destabilize the region and affect global markets. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation, as prolonged instability could have significant implications for Germany's economic outlook.

data.title: Cambridge Technology Reports Stable Operations Amid Market Concerns
data.description: Cambridge Technology announced on April 21 that its production and business activities are currently stable, with no significant issues affecting the company's stock price fluctuations. According to Jin10, the company's main business involves manufacturing communication terminal devices and high-speed optical modules for telecommunications, data communications, enterprise networks, and home networks. The industry's growth is closely linked to global communication technology advancements and the capital expenditure plans of downstream clients. However, potential adjustments in industry policies, lower-than-expected market demand, shortages of core raw materials, or significant price increases could negatively impact the company's production, product costs, and profitability.

data.title: Lawmakers Investigate Cantor's Alleged Tariff-Refund Trades Amid $127 Billion Claims
data.description: Unresolved questions regarding Cantor's alleged involvement in tariff-refund trades have resurfaced following the opening of the CAPE portal, which registered claims totaling $127 billion. According to NS3.AI, lawmakers are now seeking records related to any agreements or counterparties involved in these trades. Cantor has stated that it never executed transactions linked to the refund rights.

data.title: KelpDAO Hacker Utilizes Multiple Tools for Fund Transfers
data.description: Blockchain sleuth EmberCN posted on X that the hacker involved in the KelpDAO incident is using various platforms to transfer funds. The tools identified include UmbraCash, Chainflip, THORChain, and Symbiosis. These platforms are being utilized to move the stolen assets, highlighting the complexity and sophistication of the hacker's methods.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Potential Decline Amid Strategy's Funding Challenges
data.description: Bitcoin may face a decline below $70,000 as Strategy's financing tool, STRC, has fallen below its $100 face value, potentially affecting its ability to continue raising funds through share issuance to purchase Bitcoin. According to ChainCatcher, Strategy recently disclosed that approximately 86% of the funds for its latest purchase of 34,164 Bitcoins came from STRC financing. Historically, Bitcoin has experienced an average decline of about 30% during periods when Strategy paused its purchases. Technical analysis suggests that if the lower boundary of the flag pattern is breached, Bitcoin could test the $67,000 to $69,000 range. However, if it holds the 20-day and 50-day EMAs, there is potential for a rebound to test the $78,000 resistance level.

data.title: U.S. Trade Representative Confirms Continuation of Trump Tariffs on Mexican Companies
data.description: U.S. Trade Representative Greer has informed Mexican businesses that tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump will remain in effect. According to Odaily, this announcement underscores the ongoing trade policies between the United States and Mexico, which have been a point of contention in recent years. The tariffs, initially introduced as part of Trump's broader trade strategy, aim to address trade imbalances and protect domestic industries. Mexican companies have expressed concerns over the impact of these tariffs on their operations and the broader economic relationship between the two nations.
